messages.author: danagbemava-nc
messages.body:
If anyone else is experiencing this issue, kindly upvote the initial comment on this issue. We would also appreciate it if any 
of you experiencing this issue can provide a sample so that we can verify this. 

Keeping this issue open to track future reports of the same issue. 

Based on what I've gathered: 

- This issue is on both android and iOS https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/100522#issuecomment-1096446674
- It doesn't seem to affect platform views https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/100522#issuecomment-1090079625
- Its occurrence is sporadic and there isn't a clear way to isolate the issue
- All reports seem to indicate this happening after upgrading to flutter 2.10

Once again, if anyone is able to reproduce and isolate the cause, kindly provide a sample so that we can investigate this. 

Labeling for visibilty

messages.author: chinmaygarde
messages.body: I agree with @knopp that the errors are not expected. We recently wired up enhanced command buffer debugger in [a different renderer](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/cafc4eb02659e3cf5f6b695ecc8ae820fdcc0cee/impeller/renderer/backend/metal/command_buffer_mtl.mm#L79). I wonder if the Skia renderer command buffers have similar instrumentation in place. Usually, by the time you see "for causing prior/excessive GPU errors" in the logs the instigating failure has happened a long time ago and we need the first instance of the failure.

messages.author: chinmaygarde
messages.body: @knopp What made use suspect it was partial repaint was that this happening on both iOS and Android. But it could totally be something rendering related on all backends.

messages.author: feinstein
messages.body:
> Thanks for this. We'll try to repro, but it would also be useful to know what was happening in the app at the time. Was there anything animating, or were there any platform views on the screen, etc.?

My app is a stock trading app, so we make heavy use of websockets (and I suspect that's where some of the other crashes are coming from). The screen that presented the flickering is the first screen we open after login, which shows the user's balance, gains/losses and stock portfolio in real time.

There are no platform views, but we have some animations. There are loading animations using the [shimmer](https://pub.dev/packages/shimmer) package and also the text animates as the values change, like changes in text color (green to red and vice versa).

It's good to mention that in this screen we have a bottom navigation bar with 3 other screens. They are behind the portfolio screen, not visible but active, so it's really fast for the users to switch screens. They would have text, custom paint, images, slivers, animated widgets and styles, but no platform views.

Also, we don't use AdMob in the app.

messages.author: zanderso
messages.body:
We have not been able to reproduce this locally. Trying to summarize what is known so far:
* Started in 2.10.0, and still occurs in 3.0.0
* Several iOS 15 versions
* A range of iPhone devices
* Seems to be triggered by swiping up
* Platform views do not flicker
* Some reports that having bitcode disabled may be relevant

If anyone who is able to reproduce this locally would be willing to bisect on the flutter/flutter commits in 2.10.0, that would be very helpful. The flutter/flutter commit range is from: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/commit/fb1f8b0e38c9c34d6a1c4072288d1ea45af2bc50 to https://github.com/flutter/flutter/commit/95e555344dc746ad46ada9de9cc475042c63ec55 on the master branch.

```

so there should be no more than 10 versions to try. If that narrows things down to an Engine roll, we can then bisect on the Engine commits in the roll or just eyeball it.

messages.author: iskakaushik
messages.body:
Hi all, https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/34328 PR has [rolled into](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/106707) Flutter Framework master. If the following is added to `Info.plist` of the application, it will disable partial repaint:

```
  <key>FLTDisablePartialRepaint</key>
  <true/>
```

It would be great to know if disabling partial repaint resolves this issue. We are not yet sure if it will, but we think the severity of this issue and lack of reproducible case makes it worthwhile to try out a broad fix. It would be great if any of you experiencing this would be able to try out and post here about your findings.

messages.author: mateusfccp
messages.body: @iskakaushik Do we have any potential downsides in disabling partial repaint?

messages.author: iskakaushik
messages.body: @mateusfccp , there are some situations in which the performance of the application can regress in terms of the rasterization time. This would be most apparent in scenes where only a small part of the scene is changing. That being said, we would really appreciate if we can isolate this particular issue to partial repaint.

This happens only on iOS as far as I know. I work on a banking app so the case is next:
 - app in background
 - open the app
 - screen is blurred with BackdropFilter
 - we offer local authentication
 - user authorizes
 - remove BackdropFilter using setState inside didChangeAppLifecycleState
 - flickering happens 

 It does not happen always, it happens randomly, for example I myself couldnt reproduce it no matter how much I tried, colleague did. Not sure if this is related to platform views - but we do use local authentication which is platform view I think. 

So far what we did is, stopped using BackdropFilter and setState to show / hide it. We moved the blur to native Swift and we control it with native method channel... so far flickering has not appear YET.

messages.author: jackTang11
moffatman
messages.body:
I have seen this in my app with a `PlatformView`, but there are two interesting things:

1. The `PlatformView` is part of a widget which expands up from the bottom tab bar, so right now it's being built but is clipped by a zero-height `Stack` and should not be visible
2. The `PlatformView` itself shouldn't draw anything (`UIView` with no subviews and `backgroundColor = UIColor.clear`), it's just used as a rectangle to receive drag-and-dropped files. 

Despite that, starting at 0:15 in the following video, it starts showing up as the white rectangle in the bottom-right. That's the correct position and size, but it's not being clipped. 

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/789275/180110871-14cb60cf-1eb6-4f01-9466-fe5314f0d815.mov

Can't remember if there is `BackdropFilter` here but for sure a large part of the screen is within `ImageFiltered` with a no-op filter.

messages.author: ingmferrer
messages.body:
@zanderso So, today was the second time that this bug happened to the same tester user in the last two months.

Sadly, neither the tester user nor I can reproduce it and I can't share the commercial app code with you guys but I think something it's related to the google map widget.

The tester user have an iPhone 11 Pro with iOS 15.5, when this happened the user was interacting with a screen of sliver widgets that have a mini map using google_maps_flutter, the map just have a pin marker for a store, then the user tried to open the search page and this bug ocurred. https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6753534/181614991-3624af42-e516-4f0c-bf70-2cef3f4fccc3.MP4

I think it's related to the map widget because it's the only widget that redraws and output logs, everything else is static. Note that when the user opens the search page, the previous page with the map widget is not removed from the stack, the user can go back after searching, so the map widget is not disposed at that moment. Also, this map widget was also present the first time this ocurred. While maybe is not directly related to google map, the fact that the widget redraws and output logs because it's doing things, can be related to the bug.

I tried to reproduce the bug doing the exact same thing with an iPhone 13 Pro without luck but I noticed that the navigation transition animation from this page with the map widget visible to the search page feels very laggy compared to another page that doesn't have the map widget to the search page which feels fluid.

I'll keep an eye and maybe either optimize the map or remove it and see what happens.

messages.author: feinstein
messages.body:
Hi guys, I just want to confirm some behaviors we have seen before:

An user reported he opened the app from the background and then he tried to go to the iPhone's home screen swiping up, but then the app started to flicker and when the flickering stopped the app was frozen.

It seems to me that the key here is something happening when the app was back from background and then going back to background.

messages.author: ostk0069
messages.body: This is the research summary of our app's flickering matter. This summary is only from our developers team, not from users. **Summary (last month):** | Number of Repetitions | Devices | iOS version | Xcode version | Flutter version | | ---- | ---- |---- |---- |---- | | 15 | in most iPhones | 15.x, 16 | 13.4.1 | 3.0.4 or more | **Matching of Device & iOS version:** | Device | iOS version | | ---- | ---- | | iPhone 12 mini | 15.5 | | iPhone 13 Pro | 15.4.1 | | iPhone 12 Pro | 16 | | iPhone SE 2nd | 15.1 | | iPhone XR | 15.5 | | iPhone 12 pro | 15.4.1 | | iPhone 11 | 15.6 | **Actions just before flickering occured:** | Event | Number of Repetitions | | ---- | ---- | | showing webView by using flutter_inappwebview | 5 | | launch app from background | 6 | | other (just using app normally) | 4 | | Total | 15 | We have not found exact way to reproduce the matter yet :cry: **Package Dependency:** almost all firebase packages, video_player, audio_service, flutter_inappwebview, and more... **Console log when flickering happened:** ``` Execution of the command buffer was aborted due to an error during execution. ``` logs looks quite same with: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/100522#issuecomment-1109688949 https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/100522#issuecomment-1134784299 https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/97910

messages.author: chinmaygarde
messages.body: We continue to have conversations with the Skia team on how to debug and fix this. After https://skia-review.googlesource.com/c/skia/+/566883, we should have more intelligence on what kind of command buffer errors are encountered. We are also reviewing the logic around backgrounding and interaction with command submission. In the meanwhile, I am going to review @ostk0069 s [excellent summary](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/100522#issuecomment-1214737544) to see if we can use use that information to add to what we know about this bug. More to come.

messages.author: ostk0069
messages.body:
**Here is our additional Info about research**

We've customized our app with high load on
- CPU
- Rendering
- Memory

and monkey test the app. However, any of them did not reproduce the flickering😭 

By looking at our summary this is the high probability way to reproduce the flickering:
1. app slowdowns for some reason
2. freeze
3. make the app to background
4. make the app to foreground

> We are also reviewing the logic around backgrounding and interaction with command submission.

Just notice that our app does not have any background logic except for audio_service.

If it is possible, we would like to share the instruments log when the flickering appeared.
From a privacy point of view, could we send it by DM in twitter or something else?

This problem is the main cause of blocking our application release. 
If theres anything we could do, please let us know.

messages.author: PiotrMitkowski
messages.body:
> Hi, Flutter 3.3 no longer uses Skia but Impeller. Does this potentially mitigate the flickering? Has anyone reporting issues here faced the same on Flutter 3.3?

Flutter 3.3 still uses Skia by default, Impeller can be used only as a "feature preview", by setting some flags as mentioned [here](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/wiki/Impeller). Updating to 3.3 only didn't fix the issue in my project, but there's a possibility that enabling Impeller will do the thing.

Hi all,

There are a couple of changes to Skia that are now available on the master channel, and which we expect to be in the 3.4 beta of Flutter.

https://skia-review.googlesource.com/c/skia/+/575667 <- We suspect that this may fix a race in command buffer submission when going into the background.

https://skia-review.googlesource.com/c/skia/+/566883 <- If that change does not fix the flickering issue, then this change will give more verbose logs when command buffers fail.

@talarecc Not sure what version of Flutter you were using, but if your repro is reliable on 3.0 or 3.3 on the stable channel, it would be a big help if you could give it a shot on master channel. Thanks everyone for your patience on this issue. Hopefully we're getting close.

messages.author: jmagman
messages.body:
If you're only able to reproduce on a release version of your app, the Skia changes added os logging which we should be able to see if you reproduce, note the time it happened, then collect a sysdiagnose from the device and send it to us.  

I haven't been able to reliably collect a sysdiagnose from pressing physical buttons in a few iOS releases.  The "easiest" way I've found is to:
1. Settings > Accessibility > Touch > AssistiveTouch > Toggle on, then Single-Tap > Analytics.  When the white button appears on the screen, tap it.  You should see a notification at the top that analytics are being collected.  At that point you can turn AssistiveTouch back off.  
2. Wait ~5 minutes
3. Settings > Privacy & Security > Analytics & Improvements > Analytics Data.  Find the sysdiagnose_2022... with the matching timestamp.  Select it, choose the Share button in the top right corner.  You can Airdrop it to your Mac, or email it to yourself.  
4. Once you have the sysdiagnose on your Mac, you can peek in the logs.  Inside the sysdiagnose there is a bundle called `system_logs.logarchive`.  On the command line, run (this assumes your process is the default "Runner":
```
$ /usr/bin/log show /path/to/sysdiagnose_2022.../system_logs.logarchive --predicate "process = 'Runner'" > ~/Desktop/flutter_log.txt
```
5. Open `~/Desktop/flutter_log.txt` and confirm it contains some logging (columns with timestamps, etc), then attach it to this GitHub issue.  Make sure to also share the time you reproduced the flickering so we can narrow down where in the logs to look.

**Note sysdiagnoses contain personally identifiable information.  Be cautious sharing them.  Unless you are reproducing on a test device do not send the link to the full sysdiagnose in GitHub.**  You can reach out to us on Discord to coordinate sharing, if needed https://discord.gg/BS8KZyg.  

Thank you!

> Hi all,
> 
> We have speculatively cherry-picked the Skia patches mentioned in [#100522 (comment)](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/100522#issuecomment-1239654117) into the stable channel, and they are now available in the 3.3.7 hotfix release. messages.author: feinstein
messages.body:
@zanderso are the logs being generated with a `print`?

This is important because I think many production apps won't save prints to Crashlytics, unless they capture it in the zone and forward it to Crashlytics.

messages.author: lcdsmao
messages.body:
Hi
We collected some logs via this method https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/100522#issuecomment-1267721681 when flickering occurred.
And would like to share those logs through Discord to see whether it can get help.

There are two types of flickering we are facing now and all happened when we switch back the App from the background to the foreground:
- App crashed after flickering for a second
- App becomes normal after flickering for a second

Another question is that before using the flutter version that does not contain cherry-picks mentioned in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/100522#issuecomment-1239654117, flickering can occur during the internal transition of the app. But currently, we cannot produce flickering in this scenario via manually performing some operations or via a Monkey Test that simulates clicking and scrolling. We are curious about whether https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/100522#issuecomment-1239654117 fixes this flickering scenario.

messages.body:
We get new crash stack traces with flutter 3.3.8.

This occurred when we were doing the following steps:
- Click send email button on our app's email login page
- iPhone seems to freeze
- Go to the background and then enter the foreground of our app again
- Flickering occurred for about 1~2 second

messages.author: Hixie
messages.body: @lcdsmao @ostk0069 This issue is about screen flickering, if you are seeing a crash please file a separate bug, thanks!

messages.author: Hixie
messages.body: @zanderso do the logs from @lcdsmao above help?

messages.author: zanderso
messages.body: From Engine triage: @chinmaygarde will be taking a closer look at the crash backtraces since they may indicate that there is a race or object lifetime issue that has the same underlying cause as the GPU errors.

messages.author: jackTang11
messages.body: When will this problem be fixed? Is the fix abandoned?

messages.author: DjordjeMancic97
messages.body: So any news? It's been 2 weeks. After months of asking for logs, you get them and now what? If this issue is not one of your highest priorities then I don't know what is...

messages.author: Hixie
messages.body: Folks, this is December, a lot of our contributor are on vacation right now. Also, this bug is important, but it is not the only important bug. In general, demanding updates is a violation of our code of conduct because it doesn't feel welcoming. Rest assured that if there's an update, it will be posted here promptly. If you need it to be addressed sooner, then consider that Flutter is an open source project and that we accept contributions. There are hundreds of people contributing to Flutter in various capacities, you could be one of them.

messages.author: delfme
messages.body:
Just faced this on master with Impeller, ios 16.1.1.
App started to flash after resuming it from background.
Continuous flashing till entire screen gets a red-ish/pink-ish color and app crashed.
Unfortunately, code is closed and I cannot share, nor I can reproduce it on a sample repo.

Do I have to raise a different issue for that? 
I see issue's title still refers only to flutter 2.10.0

messages.body:
I repeated the problem again and captured the feedback information. The user's operation process: 
1. The user slides up and exits, causing a stuck state 
2. After the screen is locked, it flashes when it is opened again. 
3. It crashes after a period of time

messages.author: chinmaygarde
messages.body: From Triage: We know from @lcdsmao and @jackTang11 s backtraces that this is an over-release of a Metal command buffer. I am instrumenting our behavior now but the surface area is large. What is peculiar is that @lcdsmao backtrace points to an overrelease on the render thread while @jackTang11 s issue is on the platform thread. We now suspect that this could happen during thread merging and unmerging. This narrows down where we can look for issues. More updates incoming.

messages.author: zanderso
messages.body:
Hi everyone,

Thanks for your patience on this very difficult issue. As you've likely noticed, this thread has become quite long. It has become so long that GitHub's UI has made it essentially impossible for the team to use it. Additionally, we on the Flutter team have also not done a great job of enforcing Flutter's issue hygiene policies on this issue. This has led to a somewhat low signal to noise ratio. The combination of these factors with our inability to reproduce this issue locally requires a change in tactics. Along those lines, I'm going to close and lock this issue, and replace it with multiple issues that attempt to tease out the different situations discussed in this thread, and focus the conversations on details that will help the team fix the problems. Here they are:
- https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/123120
- https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/123121
- https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/123122
- https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/123123

Please note that these issues are focused on recent versions of Flutter as the team will not be able to back-port any fixes to older versions. Please also note that on iOS, any reproduction should be with Impeller enabled.

Thanks again for your help and continued patience!
